headlines.title: US Carries out 10th Night of Strikes After Service Members Killed
headlines.description: United States Central Command released video of its 10th night of consecutive strikes flying deep into Iran, targeting command centers, launch sites and air defenses. President Donald Trump says Iran “will pay” anytime they kill an American servicemember. Meanwhile, the State Department issued a rare global travel warning to Americans abroad after Iran sent another barrage of missiles into Jordan. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: U.S. military identifies soldiers killed in Iranian attack in Jordan
headlines.description: The Pentagon identified the soldiers as 25-year-old Tyler James Feehan of Hawaii and 19-year-old Isabella Gonzales of Texas.

headlines.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy fires his commander-in-chief after protests and major rift
headlines.description: The Ukrainian president fired his commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi on Tuesday, bowing to pressure after the removal of popular Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov sparked the fiercest leadership crisis of his presidency.

headlines.title: Child actor Kaylee Hottle killed in car crash
headlines.description: Kaylee Hottle, a child actor most well known for her role in two “Godzilla” movies, died in a car accident Tuesday in Frederick, Maryland.

headlines.title: How AI is Transforming the Way China’s Microdramas Are Made
headlines.description: Microdramas made specifically for phones have become a huge sensation in China and are finding fans in the United States. As China’s short series go global, artificial intelligence is transforming not just how their stories are told but how entertainment itself is made. NBC’s Janis Mackey Frayer reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Spain’s supremacy, Messi and Mbappé’s race for greatness, and a freak injury: World Cup quiz
headlines.description: President Donald Trump makes a call to FIFA, Scotland fans make an impression, and an upset results in a national holiday.

headlines.title: Novo Nordisk Sues Eli Lilly Over Weight Loss Drug Advertisement
headlines.description: Novo Nordisk, which makes Ozempic and Wegovy, has filed a federal lawsuit against Eli Lilly, accusing its competitor of false advertising and unfair competition. Novo Nordisk challenges national ad campaigns from Eli Lilly that compare GLP-1 drugs from both companies, saying that Lilly intentionally relies on outdated clinical trials to paint a false and misleading picture. NBC’s Christine Romans joins TODAY with the details.

headlines.title: We want to hear how Medicaid work requirements are affecting you
headlines.description: If you have lost your health coverage or are at risk of losing it, tell us your story.

headlines.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on dairy, alcohol, and cars from Canada
headlines.description: The Trump administration imposed 50% tariffs on three categories of imports from Canada, dairy, alcohol, and cars, citing discriminatory actions. NBC News' Monica Alba reports on how Trump is also considering additional tariffs due to the Canadian wildfires.

headlines.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy fires his commander-in-chief after protests and major rift
headlines.description: The Ukrainian president fired his commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi on Tuesday, bowing to pressure after the removal of popular Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov sparked the fiercest leadership crisis of his presidency.

headlines.title: 6,600 noncitizens were registered to vote in N.J. because of a ‘software error,’ governor says
headlines.description: New Jersey Democratic Gov. Mikie Sherrill said a review found that nearly 400 noncitizens cast ballots in elections between 2023 and 2024.
